title: ferrotorch-gpu: sin/cos/leaky_relu/softplus autograd-on-CUDA fails with GpuTensorNotAccessible — surfaced by phase 2.5 (#767)
description: Phase 2.5 conformance probe shows that sin / cos / leaky_relu / softplus return Err(GpuTensorNotAccessible) when called with autograd on a CUDA tensor. Root cause: each forward calls .data()? to materialize a CPU vector for the backward node's saved-state, which fails on GPU storage. Forward-only path goes through unary_map's CPU-detour-then-upload which 'works' (slowly) but the autograd path cannot. Fix shape: each grad_fn needs to either (a) hold device storage instead of a CPU vec, or (b) explicitly route through a real GPU kernel for the relevant op. Per /rust-gpu-discipline §3 the silent CPU detour is itself a policy violation that should be escalated to Err — but until the GPU kernel lands, leaky_relu/softplus need autograd-on-GPU to actually work.
